text.title: Architect → Migration → Validator + Idempotence Gate
text.summary: Split the refactor into three roles; the codemod must be idempotent; CI re-runs the codemod and fails on any new diff.

section.title: Three-role split
section.p: One agent that "does the migration" produces unreliable output. Splitting into Architect (plans the rename and the codemod), Migration (writes the codemod and runs it), and Validator (re-runs codemod on the produced branch and asserts no diff) produces a verifiable pipeline.

rule.statement: Cross-cutting refactor PRs MUST be produced by a three-role pipeline: (a) Architect agent emits a written migration plan listing files to touch and out-of-scope items; (b) Migration agent writes a re-runnable codemod and runs it; (c) Validator agent re-runs the codemod on the produced branch and asserts `git diff --quiet`. The Validator MUST run inside CI, not only locally.
rule.rationale: The idempotence assertion (`re-run produces no diff`) catches every failure mode: a missed call-site, a wrong AST matcher, a hand-edit that broke determinism. Augment Code's multi-file refactor study and OpenAI's sandboxed migration cookbook both adopt this pipeline.

example.code: # .github/workflows/refactor-validator.yml name: refactor-validator on: pull_request: paths: ['codemods/**', '**/*.ts', '**/*.tsx'] jobs: validate: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - run: npx codemod run ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }} - run: | if ! git diff --quiet; then echo "Codemod is not idempotent — re-run produced changes" git diff exit 1 fi
example.note: Idempotence gate fails the PR if a re-run produces any diff.

example.code: # No validator stage; codemod ran once and was hand-edited gh pr create --title "refactor: rename"
example.note: No proof the codemod is re-runnable; future maintainers cannot reproduce the migration.

antipattern.description: Hand-editing files inside a codemod PR after the codemod ran.
antipattern.reason: Breaks idempotence — a re-run regenerates the original; the manual edit is silently dropped on the next migration.

section.reference: PR body skeleton with codemod stats, risk, manual-touch list.
Augment Code — multi-file refactor with AI agents.
OpenAI Agents SDK — sandboxed code migration with validator.
